matter.title: Olivia B. Ellsworth v. Town of Harborwick
matter.summary: Olivia B. Ellsworth, an aggrieved abutting neighbor, brings a Rule 80B appeal in Sagadahoc County Superior Court challenging the Zoning Board of Appeals of Town of Harborwick, which granted a setback waiver for a self-storage facility; the petitioner contends the decision was an error of law and is unsupported by substantial evidence in the record.

fact_pattern.summary: Olivia B. Ellsworth, an aggrieved abutting neighbor, brings a Rule 80B appeal in Sagadahoc County Superior Court challenging the Zoning Board of Appeals of Town of Harborwick, which granted a setback waiver for a self-storage facility; the petitioner contends the decision was an error of law and is unsupported by substantial evidence in the record.
fact_pattern.narrative:
Olivia B. Ellsworth owns property in Town of Stoneport and is a abutter across a private way. On 2026-01-11, the Zoning Board of Appeals granted a setback waiver authorizing a self-storage facility on the adjoining parcel, over the petitioner's objection at the public hearing.

Olivia contends the board misapplied Land Use Ordinance § 7.4, made inadequate findings, and reached a result that the record does not support. Olivia alleges concrete harm in the form of groundwater impacts, which supplies the particularized injury required for standing as an aggrieved party.

Within forty-five days of the decision, Olivia filed this Rule 80B appeal in Bath Superior Court, naming Town of Harborwick as respondent and the permittee as a party-in-interest. Because this is an appeal on the record, the court will review the certified municipal record for error of law and substantial evidence rather than hold a trial; Olivia asks the court to reverse the decision or remand it to the board.
undisputed_facts: The Zoning Board of Appeals of Town of Harborwick granted a setback waiver for a self-storage facility on 2026-01-11.
Olivia B. Ellsworth owns abutting property and appeared in opposition before the board.
Olivia filed this appeal in Bath Superior Court under M.R. Civ. P. 80B.
disputed_facts: Whether Olivia has standing as an aggrieved party who suffered a particularized injury distinct from the general public.
Whether the board committed an error of law in applying Land Use Ordinance § 7.4.
Whether the board's findings are supported by substantial evidence in the record.
Whether the board's findings of fact are adequate and free of bias or conflict of interest.

timeline.event: The Zoning Board of Appeals granted a setback waiver for a self-storage facility.
Rule 80B notice of appeal filed in Bath Superior Court.

third_parties.name: Lillian R. Kingsley
third_parties.relevance: Administers the ordinance, reviewed the application, and is the record-keeper for the board's proceedings.

third_parties.name: Maria K. Kingsley
third_parties.relevance: Presided over the hearing and signed the decision now under appeal.

third_parties.name: Maria T. Coe
third_parties.relevance: Prepared the plans and engineering submissions the board relied on in granting relief.

third_parties.name: Omar C. Roe
third_parties.relevance: Appeared and objected at the hearing and shares the petitioner's concerns about the project.

third_parties.name: Theodore S. Larkspur
third_parties.relevance: Advised the board at the hearing and defends the decision on appeal.

exchanges.question: What did the board decide, and how does it affect you?
exchanges.answer: The Zoning Board of Appeals granted a setback waiver for a self-storage facility right next to me. I live in Town of Stoneport and my property adjoins the parcel. I spoke against it at the hearing, but they approved it anyway.

exchanges.question: How does this project harm you specifically?
exchanges.answer: It is not just a general gripe. I am looking at groundwater impacts. That is a real, particular injury to my property, not something every resident of town shares.

exchanges.question: Why do you think the board got it wrong?
exchanges.answer: They did not really apply Land Use Ordinance § 7.4 the way it is written, and their findings were thin. I do not think the record supports what they did.

exchanges.question: When was the decision, and are we in time to appeal?
exchanges.answer: It was decided on 2026-01-11. I know there is a 45-day clock under the rules, and I came to you right away so we would not blow the deadline.

exchanges.question: How does this kind of appeal work?
exchanges.answer: I understand it is not a brand-new trial. The Superior Court looks at the town's record and decides if the board made a legal error or had no real evidence. I just want it reversed, or at least sent back.

exchanges.question: What outcome are you hoping for?
exchanges.answer: Stop or rein in the project and protect my neighborhood. If the court will not reverse outright, I would take a remand that forces the board to do it properly.

goals: Reverse or vacate the Zoning Board of Appeals's decision granted a setback waiver for a self-storage facility.
In the alternative, remand to the board for proper findings and a record-supported decision.
Protect the petitioner's property and neighborhood from groundwater impacts.
priorities: Establishing standing as an aggrieved abutter with a particularized injury.
Showing error of law and the absence of substantial evidence within the record-review constraints.
Preserving the issues that were raised before the board below.
desired_outcomes: A judgment reversing the board's decision, or a remand for adequate findings.
A result that meaningfully limits or conditions a self-storage facility.
non_starters: A disposition that lets the unsupported decision stand without review of the record.

constraints.budget: Limited but committed; willing to fund a trial-of-the-facts motion only if clearly necessary.
constraints.timeline: Wants the appeal briefed and argued promptly, mindful of the 45-day filing deadline already met.

issues.issue: Whether the board's findings of fact were adequate to permit meaningful review
issues.governing_law: M.R. Civ. P. 80B; Maine administrative-law findings requirement
sub_questions: Did the board make findings sufficient to support its conclusion?
Can the court conduct meaningful review on the findings made?
issues.client_position: The board's findings are conclusory and inadequate, warranting reversal or remand.

issues.issue: Whether the petitioner has standing as an aggrieved party with a particularized injury
issues.governing_law: Adelman v. Town of Baldwin, 2000 ME 91; M.R. Civ. P. 80B
sub_questions: Does the petitioner suffer a particularized injury distinct from the general public?
Does abutter status and the alleged harm confer standing to appeal?
issues.client_position: As a abutter across a private way facing groundwater impacts, the petitioner has a particularized injury sufficient for standing.

issues.issue: Whether bias or a conflict of interest of a board member tainted the decision
sub_questions: Did a board member have a disqualifying conflict of interest?
Did bias deprive the petitioner of a fair and impartial hearing?
issues.client_position: A board member's conflict or bias deprived the petitioner of a fair hearing.

issues.issue: Whether the board committed an error of law in applying the ordinance
issues.governing_law: M.R. Civ. P. 80B; Land Use Ordinance § 7.4
sub_questions: Did the board misinterpret or misapply the governing ordinance?
Is the court's review of the legal question de novo?
issues.client_position: The board misapplied Land Use Ordinance § 7.4, an error of law the court reviews without deference.

issues.issue: Whether the board's findings are supported by substantial evidence in the record
sub_questions: Is there substantial evidence in the record supporting the board's findings?
Did the board ignore record evidence to the contrary?
issues.client_position: The record lacks substantial evidence for the board's key findings.

expert_opinions.topic: Diminution in the value of the petitioner's property
expert_opinions.opinion: The approved project would measurably diminish the value of the abutting property. (Such valuation evidence ordinarily requires a trial of the facts to be considered on a Rule 80B appeal.)
expert_opinions.basis: Comparable-sales analysis and inspection of the abutting property.

expert_opinions.topic: Traffic and access impacts of the project on the abutting way
expert_opinions.opinion: The project will generate traffic impacts the record does not adequately address. (Relevant to a trial-of-the-facts motion; otherwise the court is confined to the record before the board.)
expert_opinions.basis: Trip-generation analysis and review of the site plans of record.

expert_opinions.topic: Application of the ordinance and planning standards to the project
expert_opinions.opinion: The board's decision departs from the ordinance and accepted planning practice. (Offered chiefly to support a motion for a trial of the facts; in pure record review such opinion may be limited to the existing record.)
expert_opinions.basis: Review of the certified record, the ordinance, and the application materials.

evidence.title: Certified municipal record and board decision with findings
evidence.description: The certified record assembled by the municipality, including the written decision and findings of fact under review.

evidence.title: Board meeting minutes and hearing audio
evidence.description: Minutes and the audio recording of the hearing at which the board acted.

evidence.title: Variance / site-plan application and plans
evidence.description: The application, narrative, and engineered plans the permittee submitted to the board.

evidence.title: Text of the governing ordinance
evidence.description: The full text of Land Use Ordinance § 7.4 as in effect at the time of the decision.

evidence.title: Photographs of the site and abutting property
evidence.description: Photographs depicting the parcel, the abutting property, and the alleged impacts.

evidence.title: Staff and engineering reports
evidence.description: Code enforcement, staff, and peer-review engineering reports in the record.

evidence.title: Petitioner's objection letters
evidence.description: Written objections the petitioner submitted to the board before and during the hearing.

amounts.label: Estimated cost of the Rule 80B appeal
amounts.basis: Attorney fees and costs for a record-review appeal.

amounts.label: Estimated diminution in the petitioner's property value
amounts.basis: Appraiser's estimate of value loss attributable to the project.

causes_of_action.claim: Error of law in applying the ordinance
causes_of_action.governing_law: M.R. Civ. P. 80B; Land Use Ordinance § 7.4
elements: Misinterpretation or misapplication of the governing ordinance
Reviewed by the court as a question of law
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Reversal or vacatur of the board's decision

causes_of_action.claim: Decision unsupported by substantial evidence in the record
elements: A board decision resting on findings of fact
The absence of substantial evidence in the record to support those findings
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Reversal of the decision as unsupported by the record

causes_of_action.claim: Abuse of discretion and inadequate findings of fact
elements: A duty to make adequate findings
Conclusory or inadequate findings that preclude meaningful review
An exercise of discretion outside the bounds permitted by the ordinance
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Remand for adequate findings or reversal

causes_of_action.claim: Procedural error or bias depriving the petitioner of a fair hearing
elements: A right to a fair and impartial hearing
A disqualifying conflict, bias, or procedural defect
causes_of_action.relief_sought: Vacatur and remand for a new hearing before an impartial board

affirmative_defenses.basis: The board's findings rest on substantial evidence in the certified record and are entitled to deference.
The petitioner suffered no particularized injury distinct from the general public. Adelman v. Town of Baldwin, 2000 ME 91.
The notice of appeal was not filed within the 45-day deadline. 30-A M.R.S. § 2691(3)(G); M.R. Civ. P. 80B.

motions.title: Motion to stay the permit pending appeal
motions.relief: A stay halting work under the permit while the appeal is pending.

motions.title: Motion to supplement the record
motions.relief: Leave to add omitted materials to the certified record.

motions.title: Motion for a trial of the facts under M.R. Civ. P. 80B(d)
motions.relief: Leave to take evidence beyond the record on a disputed factual issue.

motions.title: Motion to specify and assemble the record
motions.relief: An order directing the municipality to assemble and certify the complete administrative record.

docket.entry: Notice of appeal filed under M.R. Civ. P. 80B.
Administrative record certified and filed by the municipality.
Reply brief filed and the appeal taken under advisement on the briefs.
Oral argument scheduled before the Superior Court.
